source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Calculate $A_{F}=\frac{V_{0}}{V_{i}}$, the amplification of the circuit with feedback shown in Fig. 3.55. $A_{0}=\frac{V_{0}}{V}$, the amplification without feedback, is large and negative. The input resistance to $A_{0}$ is much greater than $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ and the output resistance is much less than $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$. Discuss the dependence of $A_{\mathrm{F}}$ on $A_{0}$.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $A_{\mathrm{F}} \approx -\frac{R_{2}}{R_{1}}$, so the amplification is independent of $A_{0}$ and determined by the resistor ratio $R_{1}/R_{2}$.
extraction_notes: images field contains an in-memory PIL image representation string, not a concrete file path or URL

metadata.reasoning_chain:
As $A_{0}$ is large and the input resistance is much greater than $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$, while the output resistance is much less than $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$, we can consider the circuit with feedback as an ideal amplifier. Taking $i_{1}=-i_{2}$, then
$$V_{i}-V=- rac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}\left(V_{0}-Vight)$$
or
$$ rac{V_{i}}{V_{0}}- rac{V}{V_{0}}=- rac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}\left(1- rac{V}{V_{0}}ight),$$
putting
$$A_{F}= rac{V_{0}}{V_{i}},\quad A_{0}= rac{V_{0}}{V},$$
the above becomes
$$ rac{1}{A_{F}}=- rac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}+\left(1+ rac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}ight) rac{1}{A_{0}},$$
giving
$$A_{F}= rac{1}{- rac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}+\left(1+ rac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}ight) rac{1}{A_{0}}}.$$ 
As $A_{0}$ is large, $A_{F}\approx - rac{R_{2}}{R_{1}}$.
